sections.title: Performance Analysis — Distill (book_shorts)
sections.content: This is a static-content React app (no backend/DB), so classic "N+1 SQL" doesn't apply literally — but there's a direct analogue: **components that independently re-derive or re-subscribe to shared state per list item**. Here's what I found, ranked by impact.

sections.title: 1. Root cause: the store notifies ALL subscribers on ANY write (biggest win)
2. `BookCard` re-render/recompute fan-out (Library.tsx:29-32)
3. `bookStats`/`categoryOf`/`getBook`/`booksInCategory` — static data, recomputed every call
4. Chapter content re-fetched and re-parsed on every visit (no caching)
5. `Markdown` component re-parses on every render (BookMap.tsx)
6. Minor: `sanitize()` re-runs array dedup/sort on every read
sections.content: `src/lib/progress.ts:20-31` — `sanitize` does `new Set(...)` + `.sort()` on `chaptersRead` every time it's called, including inside `latestProgress` for every book in `ContinueStrip` on every Library render. Low cost at current data sizes but compounds with #1's over-notification. Once the selector fix (#1) lands, this stops re-running on unrelated updates.

sections.title: Memory leaks — none found
sections.content: Checked all `useEffect` hooks in `Reader.tsx`, `Library.tsx`, `App.tsx`: every `addEventListener` has a matching cleanup, and the chapter-load effect uses an `alive` flag to guard against setting state after unmount. `store.ts`'s listener `Set` is properly cleaned up via the unsubscribe returned from `subscribe`. No dangling timers, intervals, or unclosed subscriptions.

sections.title: Priority order
sections.content:
1. Selector-based `useStore` (#1) — fixes the root cause, cheap change, biggest blast radius.
2. Memoize `bookStats`/index `getBook`/`booksInCategory` (#3) — trivial, static data.
3. Cache `loadChapter` (#4) — noticeable on repeat chapter navigation.
4. `React.memo` + `useMemo` on `BookCard`/`MapChapterCard`/`Markdown` (#2, #5) — pairs with #1 for full effect.

None of this requires new dependencies — all fixes are local to `src/lib/store.ts`, `src/lib/content.ts`, `src/lib/markdown.tsx`, and the two list-rendering components. Want me to implement these?

codeBlocks.code:
function BookCard({ book, step }: { book: Book; step?: number }) {
  const all = useAllProgress()        // subscribes to the WHOLE progress store
  const progress = all[book.id]
  const stats = bookStats(book)       // recomputed from scratch every render
const MapChapterCard = memo(function MapChapterCard({ bookId, chapter, read, available }: {...}) { ... })
